specific_location: Barr Lake State Park banding station
description: The upperparts were grayish olive, and the underparts were white. The flanks were also grayish olive and not contrasting with the white breast and belly. The upper breast had a slight buffy wash. The olive-brown breast spots, which were restricted mainly to the upper breast, were more distinctive in the center of the upper breast whereas the other spots clustered together to form blotches. The bill was dark except for the base of the lower mandible, which was pale with a yellow tinge and only extending just past the nares. A distinctive, yellow gape was also present. This bird also had a slight hint of a pale eye ring. The wing chord was 100 mm and the tail 70 mm. This GCTH belonged to the subspecies C.m. minimus.Behaviors: Normal behaviors of the Gray-cheeked Thrush were not observed during its capture and banding.Call: not applicablePlumage: grayish olive upperparts and spotted whi
